fields.label: Target DocTypes (list, one Custom DocPerm per entry)
Desk access
fields.rationale: Almost every business role needs Desk. Toggling desk_access on an existing role re-evaluates user_type (System User vs Website User) for every user assigned the role on the next cache clear. Set 0 only for portal-only personas (Customer, Supplier style) that never log into Desk. The Guest role is special-cased to desk_access=0 regardless of what you set.

fields.label: Require 2FA
fields.rationale: Default off. Flip on only when the user explicitly requests heightened security for this role - it adds a 2FA prompt on every login for every holder of the role.

fields.label: Restrict to Domain
fields.rationale: Optional. When set to a Domain name (e.g. Manufacturing), the role is hidden unless that Domain is enabled on the site. Empty means the role is available regardless of active Domains.

fields.label: Permission level
fields.rationale: Permlevel 0 covers the whole document. Higher permlevels gate field-level perms: you MUST also have a level-0 row for the same role (the controller rejects a higher-level perm without a level-0 companion unless the role is ALL_USER_ROLE or SYSTEM_USER_ROLE). V1 defaults to 0 unless the prompt names a specific level.

fields.label: Action flags (comma list of key=0|1)
fields.rationale: Default grants read+write+create+delete+print+email+export+report+share. Controller enforces cascades: submit=1 requires create=1 OR write=1 at the same or lower level; cancel=1 requires submit=1; amend=1 requires submit=1 AND cancel=1; import=1 requires the target DocType to carry allow_import=1. Never grant write=1 with read=0 - Frappe needs read for write to take effect. Leave submit/cancel/amend at 0 unless the target DocType is submittable and the role should approve.
